architecture.summary: The system combines open-source LLMs (Flan-T5-Large, LLaMA-2-7B, Mistral-7B) with Retrieval-Augmented Generation (RAG) and fine-tuning on medical datasets. Information flows from medical corpora (Meadow-MedQA, MedMCQA) into a RAG database that augments model context during inference; models are also fine-tuned on the same medical data. The system evaluates four configurations: base models only, base+RAG, fine-tuned only, and fine-tuned+RAG. Two answer methodologies are tested: Type A (direct multiple-choice selection) and Type B (language generation with confidence scores over choices).
trained_components: LLM fine-tuning on medical datasets (Flan-T5-Large, LLaMA-2-7B, Mistral-7B)

biblio.title: Systematic Analysis of Retrieval-Augmented Generation-Based LLMs for Medical Chatbot Applications

claims.statement: Fine-tuning and RAG are crucial for improved performance in the medical domain
Type A (direct choice selection) outperforms Type B (language generation and probability confidence score generation of choices)
The paper evaluates open-source LLMs in computationally resource-constrained environments for medical chatbot applications
